//: Pylance / Pyright config. mypy --strict is the source of truth for type checking; this file makes the IDE agree with it. Do not duplicate strictness here that mypy doesn't enforce.

executionEnvironments.//: Mirrors `pythonpath = ['.', 'services/py']` from pyproject.toml so absolute imports like `from proto.codegen import ...` and `from tests._util import ...` resolve in the IDE the same way pytest sees them.

//useLibraryCodeForTypes: Many of our deps (pyarrow, filelock, @grpc/...) ship partial or no stubs. Reading their source for inferred types beats showing a wall of red in the editor.
//reportMissingTypeStubs: mypy --strict already enforces type discipline at our boundaries; the IDE warning here is pure noise for third-party libs that ship partial / missing stubs.
//reportMissingImports: Stays on — a missing import is almost always a real bug.
